suares_cipher_of_genesis.description: A comprehensive database of the Qabalistic Code letter-numbers, detailing their symbols, numerical values, structural formulas, and ontological meanings as interpreted by Carlo Suarès.

structural_levels.Archetypes: Numbers 1 to 9 represent the archetypes or templates.
structural_levels.Existences: Numbers 10 to 90 describe the process of the archetypes in their factual, conditioned existence and physical form.
structural_levels.Cosmic: Numbers 100 to 900 represent the exalted archetypes in their cosmic manifestation.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The unthinkable life-death, abstract principle of all that is and all that is not. It is the creative immanence, spontaneous, always fresh and new; imperishable pulsation of life.
autiot_symbols.imagery: A hammering of the invisible world and the march of time. An intermittent spark or discontinuous pulsation buried in all containers.

connections.relationship: Yod is the projection of Aleph into temporal continuity; they are partners playing the game of life versus existence.
Qof is Aleph exalted in its principle, cosmically deathless.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The archetype of all 'dwellings' and containers. It is the physical support without which nothing is. It represents the conditioning of life and the life of the conditioning.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The protective shell, the body, or the house that contains our consciousness.

connections.relationship: Kaf is the archetype of Bayt-in-existence, ready to receive all that comes.
Raysh is the cosmic container of all existence.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The organic movement of every Bayt animated by Aleph.
autiot_symbols.imagery: Uncontrolled functional action and the organic motion within containers.

connections.relationship: Lammed is the controlled connecting agent of this movement.
Sheen is the universal, progressive enlargement of this movement into cosmic breath.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The physical existence and response to life. In inorganic structures, it acts as its own resistance to destruction.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The physical resistance of structures that protects the life inside.

connections.relationship: Mem provides the maternal waters where this resistance finds its origin.
Tav is the ultimate exaltation of this cosmic resistance to life-death.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The archetype of universal life. When conferred upon Dallet, it initiates the game of existence.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The breath of universal life uniting with intermittent life-death.

connections.relationship: Noun is universal life condensed into individual existences.
Final Kaf is universal life exalted cosmically in human beings.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The male fertilizing agent that impregnates, acting as the direct result of Hay upon Dallet.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The connecting agent or copulative link maintaining grammatical and cosmic connections.

connections.relationship: Sammekh acts as the female counterpart to Waw's male fertility.
Final Mem is the cosmic achievement of this fruitfulness.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The achievement of vital impregnation, opening the field of every possible possibility.
autiot_symbols.imagery: A dynamic opening toward infinite potentials, acting against static existence.

connections.relationship: Ayn is the source and vision of these possibilities.
Final Noun exalts this into the cosmic principle of indetermination.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The sphere of storage for all undifferentiated energy and unstructured substance.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The most unevolved state of energy, acting as a primordial reservoir.

connections.relationship: Phay is the existential manifestation of this primordial substance.
Final Phay is the cosmic reserve of this unstructured energy.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The archetype of the primeval female energy, which draws life from Hhayt to build structures.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The simplest, most primitive cell constructing organized forms.

connections.relationship: Tsadde is the existential progression of this female structural energy.
Final Tsadde represents the transfigured, cosmic symbols of womanhood.

autiot_symbols.meaning: Existence which both betrays and satisfies life. It provides continuity in the duration of that which duration destroys.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The hand; the temporal projection of Aleph playing the perpetual game of existence against timelessness.

connections.relationship: Yod is Aleph's opposite, capturing it in finite time and space.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The archetype of Bayt-in-existence; the capacity to receive all that comes.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The hollow of the hand, prepared to hold or shape physical reality.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The controlled connecting agent linking organic movement.
autiot_symbols.imagery: Functional, physiological movement translating energy into structural action.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The basic attribute of matter; the maternal waters where all life originates.
autiot_symbols.imagery: A metamorphosis offering resistance to life, giving birth to living beings.

autiot_symbols.meaning: Universal life (Hay) condensed into individual, factual existences.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The seed or individualized spark of life functioning within a specific container.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The female agent of fertility in existence.
autiot_symbols.imagery: Fruitfulness enclosed within physical containers.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The principle of indetermination and the source of vision.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The eye; an opening that maintains every possible possibility of unfoldment against static bounds.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The existential sphere of undifferentiated energy and unstructured substance.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The unformulated primal material operating within conditioned reality.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The progression ascending from primitive cells to complex structures.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The construction of forms and the structural energy of womanhood.

autiot_symbols.meaning: Aleph exalted in its principle yet acting through its projection, against itself, thereby becoming cosmically deathless.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The mythical destroyer of illusions; cosmic timelessness blended with existence.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The totality of the Universe, the cosmic dwelling of life retaining manifestation.
autiot_symbols.imagery: Interstellar space, myriads of stars and planets acting as the physical support for Aleph.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The prodigious cosmic motion; the universal movement of everything that exists.
autiot_symbols.imagery: A powerful breath or 'spirit of God' which vivifies, carries away, and acts as a cosmic steam roller against static structures.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The cosmic resistance to the life-breath which animates it. Without it, life could not come into existence.
autiot_symbols.imagery: The ultimate anvil of the resistant container holding the evolutionary process.

autiot_symbols.meaning: The cosmic exaltation of individual existence and universal life as they unfold in human beings.
The cosmic achievement of fruitfulness in both the immaterial intelligence and the flesh.
The first and ultimate principle at stake in the universe: the freedom of indetermination where life itself is at stake.
The cosmic scale of primordial substance; the deepest unfathomed reserve of unstructured energy or unconscious darkness.
The transfigured and exalted symbols of womanhood, both social and mythical.
